ocr.text: The automotive semiconductor landscape is undergoing significant transformation, driven by advancements in process node
migration and the growing adoption of advanced packaging technologies.
ProcessNode  UnitsSold  Revenue(CHF)  Headcount  Branches  MarketShare  GrowthRate
As illustrated in the accompanying tables, companies are progressively transitioning from established nodes to more advanced
architectures, with the 7nm process node projected to yield a cost reduction in manufacturing as evidenced by 7nm's Cost of
Goods Sold at CHF450M.
AutomotiveChipSupplyStatus  SalesandProduction  Q12024
UnitsSold  ProductionVolume  SalesRevenue  MarketShare  Headcount
20000  CHF120M  35%  600  NorthAmerica  15000  30000  [HIDDEN]
Furthermore, investment in research and development remains essential; for instance, R&D Investment has been allocated at
CHF180M, demonstrating a commitment to innovation in this sector.
AdvancedPackagingMetrics  SalesPerformance  EmployeeStatistics
UnitsSold  Revenue(CHF)  CostofGoodsSold  GrossMargin(%)  Headcount  NumberofBranches  R&DInvestment
[HIDDEN]  155M  92M  [HIDDEN]  3050  11  30M
Additionally, the automotive chip supply status indicates an increasing demand, with Units Sold reaching 52,000 units in recent
analyses. The importance of Europe in this context cannot be understated, as it is expected to capture a significant share of the
market, with projections indicating that 25% of total automotive chips will be produced in the region. Notably, advanced
packaging adoption is also on the rise, aligning with the industry's shift towards more integrated and efficient semiconductor
solutions. Consequently, stakeholders are urged to monitor these metrics closely, including the gross margin percentage, which
currently stands at 38%, to effectively navigate this evolving market landscape.
